frameworks.name: population coding
frameworks.one_line: characterising dlPFC population activity across behavioural contexts.

frameworks.name: prospective/retrospective neural coding
frameworks.one_line: neurons represent past saccades and planned future saccades.

core_question: How does primate dlPFC population activity represent past and upcoming saccadic movements across diverse behavioural contexts?
key_assumptions: saccades provide a common motor event across tasks
population recordings reveal cognitive correlates beyond single-neuron analyses
manipulations: different training stages across multiple tasks
perceptual discrimination vs reward-based decision tasks
delayed reward-eligible saccades vs other saccades
key_findings: reliable strong modulation tied to retrospective representation of most recent saccade across all contexts
prospective planning-like activity restricted to task-related delayed saccades eligible for reward
highly structured link between prospective and retrospective representations
only prospective representations modulated by recent behavioural context
interpretations: structured prospective-retrospective link may reflect a hard-wired feature of saccade responses in dlPFC
retrospective saccade coding is context-invariant while prospective coding is context-sensitive
